channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Unsupervised Machine Learning of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Case Reports Reveals Decades of Evolving Research Themes.
item.description: Journal of gastrointestinal and liver diseases : JGLD - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) case reports provide rich longitudinal insights but have rarely been analyzed using quantitative text-mining approaches. This study applied unsupervised machine lea...

item.title: Polystyrene nanoplastics trigger ferroptosis to aggravate ulcerative colitis: Integrated multi-database bioinformatics, machine learning, and experimental validation.
item.description: Chemico-biological interactions - Polystyrene is a common pollutant in microplastics, which can enter the human body in many ways. At the same time, the incidence of ulcerative colitis (UC) has been rising, and there is evidence th...

item.title: Implementation of a prospective video databank for artificial intelligence model development, validation and clinical outcomes research in gastrointestinal endoscopy.
item.description: Gastrointestinal endoscopy - High-quality, annotated datasets are fundamental to clinical research and artificial intelligence (AI) model development. Existing endoscopic databanks are predominantly retrospective, image-based,...

item.title: Metabolic Profiling of the Gut-Brain Axis in a Murine Model of Ulcerative Colitis with Raman Spectroscopy.
item.description: ACS applied materials & interfaces - The gut-brain axis (GBA) enables a bidirectional crosstalk between the enteric and central nervous systems and plays a critical role in inflammatory diseases of the gut. This study is the first dem...

item.title: Coal Dust-Mimetic Carbon Nanoparticles Induce Colonic Epithelial Cell Senescence via p16/p21 Dual Pathways and Promote Colonic Inflammatory Response.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease among coal miners is globally higher than that in the general population; however, the underlying pathogenic mechanisms remain unclear. Given that the co...

item.title: Mapping the evolutionary landscape of intestinal organoids: from self-organization biology to precision medicine.
item.description: Stem cell research & therapy - Review - The advent of intestinal organoids has fundamentally transformed biomedical research by providing a physiological ex vivo model that recapitulates native tissue architecture. Despite an exponential...

item.title: Evolving Research Hotspots and Emerging Trends in Atopic Dermatitis and Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Bibliometric Analysis.
item.description: Clinical, cosmetic and investigational dermatology - The number of people affected by atopic dermatitis (AD) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues to rise, increasing the disease burden. These conditions are epidemiologically linked and shar...

item.title: HSP27 in autoimmune diseases: Context-dependent regulation and clinical potential.
item.description: Biochimica et biophysica acta. Molecular basis of disease - Review - Heat shock protein 27 (HSP27) is a crucial member of the small heat shock protein family with a molecular weight of approximately 27 kDa, playing key roles in cellular stress response, protein home...

item.title: Thalidomide for CGD-related inflammatory bowel disease: A randomized, double-blind trial.
item.description: Journal of human immunity - Chronic granulomatous disease-related inflammatory bowel disease (CGD-IBD) requires effective and safe therapies, as conventional immunosuppressants increase infection risk. Thalidomide, with anti-...

item.title: Paeoniae decoction attenuates chronic colitis-associated intestinal fibrosis by suppressing TGF-β/Smad-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition and remodeling the metabolic microenvironment.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Paeoniae Decoction (PD) is a classic Chinese medicine formula for treating intestinal diseases. However, its direct intervention effect on intestinal fibrosis and the underlying molecular mechanism...

item.title: Oral Delivery of Mesenchymal Stem Cell-Derived Extracellular Vesicles To Treat Intestinal Inflammation.
item.description: ACS applied materials & interfaces - Despite advances in therapy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), current treatments are associated with poor clinical outcomes and systemic side effects. Mesenchymal stem cell-derived extracellula...

item.title: Epidemiology and clinical profile of inflammatory bowel disease in Kuwait: a nationwide tertiary care (SEEF-IBD) study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - The prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues to rise globally, with notable shifts in its epidemiological patterns. However, data on characteristics of IBD in Kuwait remain limited....

item.title: Systematic immunological evaluation in adult patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) has been frequently reported in patients with primary immunodeficiency; however, the frequency and pattern of immunological abnormalities among adult patients with ...

item.title: The burden and unique patterns of extraintestinal manifestations in Chinese inflammatory bowel disease: a nationwide multicenter investigation.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) are a significant disease burden in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, large-scale epidemiological data from the Chinese population remain scarce. This...

item.title: Uveitis in spondyloarthritis: clinical patterns and differences between axial and peripheral forms.
item.description: Clinical rheumatology - The association between uveitis and spondyloarthritis is well established. However, its characterization across the different types of spondyloarthritis remains limited in the literature. Therefore...

item.title: Clinical Decision-Making and Multidisciplinary Management of Peristomal Pyoderma Gangrenosum in Stage IVB Rectal Cancer: A Case Report-Corticosteroid Response but Fatal Cancer Progression.
item.description: Reports (MDPI) - Case report - Background and Clinical Significance: Peristomal pyoderma gangrenosum (PPG) is a rare subtype of pyoderma gangrenosum, most commonly associated with inflammatory bowel disease or haematologic disor...

item.title: Lactylation-Related Genes in Ulcerative Colitis: A Multiomics Mendelian Randomization Study for Therapeutic Target Discovery.
item.description: Human mutation - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic and nonspecific intestinal inflammatory disease, has seen a rising incidence rate worldwide year by year. Its pathogenesis remains incompletely understood. Lactyl...

item.title: Exploring the genetic correlation between inflammatory bowel disease and psychiatric disorders: insights from genome-wide association studies.
item.description: European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and psychiatric disorders exhibit complex comorbid patterns, with anxiety and depressive disorders being significantly more prevalent in IBD patients compared to th...

item.title: Global trends in the burden of inflammatory bowel disease from 1990 to 2021: socioeconomic and sex-specific disparities with exploratory mendelian randomization analyses.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) shows substantial variation in burden across regions and populations worldwide. However, patterns of IBD b3urden across levels of socio-demographic development rema...

item.title: Systematic assessment for potential endogenous normalizers for microRNA analysis in fecal samples.
item.description: Scientific reports - MicroRNAs (miRNA) are considered promising biomarkers for inflammatory and neoplastic diseases in fecal samples, but despite reproducible miRNA detection, there is no consensus on appropriate norma...
